mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate signals an accommodative monetary policy that reduces borrowing costs and boosts equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A labor market slack indicator, where the unemployment rate exceeds 8%, signals weak economic demand and potential recessionary risks, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 8%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in GDP growth (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) signals strong demand conditions, which drive corporate earnings and lead to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of instances when both GDPC1 and HOUST rise by > 1% sequentially.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, rising federal funds rates signal contractionary policy that increases discount rates and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25% in 1 month.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL exceeding 225, signals potential central bank intervention through rate hikes, which increases borrowing costs and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds 225.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates robust supply-side activity, which supports corporate profits and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of instances when INDPRO rises by > 1% sequentially.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) and high inflation (CPIAUCSL) creates a stagflationary environment, which erodes corporate margins and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.5% and CPIAUCSL > 225.

mechanism_narrative: Strong employment growth, as indicated by PAYEMS exceeding 130,000, signals a tight labor market and rising corporate hiring, which boosts consumer spending and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of instances when PAYEMS exceeds 130,000.
